ChangeRequest.name: [IReF] Materiality threshold and technical issue indicator needed for Past due date
ChangeRequest.comment:
<!-- See: documentation on [Issues in GitLab](https://gitlab.sofa.dev/sdd-bird-team/bird-logical-data-model/-/wikis/Issues-in-GitLab) -->
### Overview Past Due date in FINREP is differently interpreted than in AnaCredit. AnaCredit does not do the materiality threshold. And technical past due (because of a technical issue you can ignore it in FINREP, not in AnaCredit). EU 2018/171.
### Contact person @Olivia.Hauet @Arjan.Bos
### Description of change request The reporting of the `Past due date` in AnaCredit is very straightforward. No matter the reason or the amount due or the amount of time past, the moment an _Instrument_ payment is past its due date, that moment becomes the `Past due date` for that _Instrument_.
In FINREP, the interpretation of the moment of past due, must take the materiality threshold into account - how material is the fact that the payment is late. There is a threshold for this, and the moment the late payment is over that threshold, the `Past due date` starts. For retail exposures, the threshold is EUR 100 or equivalent, for non-retail exposures this threshold differs per _Asset class_.
FINREP also takes the `Technical issue indicator` into account. Was the reason for the late payment because of a technical issue (and thus presumably outside of the control of the _Debtor_), then it does not count as past due and there is no `Date past due`.
Therefore BIRD needs to consider the inclusion of the following attributes: - `Materiality threshold amount` - `Technical issue indicator`
And perhaps model the distinction between retail and non-retail exposures and its impact on the materiality threshold. - Retail / non-retail exposure indicator - Asset class
### Legal reference [Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2018/171 of 19 October 2017 on supplementing Regulation (EU) No 575/2013 of the European Parliament and of the Council with regard to regulatory technical standards for the materiality threshold for credit obligations past due (Text with EEA relevance. )](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32018R0171)
### Summary of impacted LDM objects/attributes Impacted are all objects where the `Date past due` is located in the LDM: - Credit facility risk data - Debt security - Past due financial asset instrument
